task_completion.description: Verify the site title and tagline have been restored to their original values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress 'Settings > General' page, the 'Site Title' input field contains exactly 'My WordPress Blog' and the 'Tagline' input field contains exactly 'A WordPress blog for testing and demonstrations'.

task_completion.description: Verify the site timezone has been restored to America/Los_Angeles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress 'Settings > General' page, the 'Timezone' dropdown is set to 'Los Angeles' or 'America/Los_Angeles'.

task_completion.description: Verify public user registration is disabled or the default role is not administrator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress 'Settings > General' page, either the 'Membership' checkbox ('Anyone can register') is unchecked, OR the 'New User Default Role' dropdown is set to a non-administrator role (e.g., 'Subscriber').

task_completion.description: Verify that comments require manual moderation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress 'Settings > Discussion' page, under the 'Before a comment appears' section, the checkbox for 'Comment must be manually approved' is checked.

task_completion.description: Verify the URL structure uses SEO-friendly permalinks instead of plain numeric IDs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress 'Settings > Permalinks' page, a radio button other than 'Plain' is selected (e.g., 'Post name', 'Day and name', 'Month and name', or 'Custom Structure').

task_completion.description: Verify 'admin' is the only administrator account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress 'Users > All Users' page, the user 'service_worker' is either deleted or no longer has the 'Administrator' role. The user 'admin' should be the only account listed with the 'Administrator' role.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the WordPress Admin GUI rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the WordPress web interface in a browser. There should be no terminal windows showing WP-CLI commands (e.g., `wp option update` or `wp user delete`) being executed.

integrity.description: Verify no external security plugins were used to fix the issues.
integrity.visual_evidence: The WordPress sidebar and Plugins page should not show newly installed third-party security plugins (e.g., Wordfence, Sucuri, Solid Security) being used to manage these settings.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual live application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser rendering the WordPress dashboard with a valid URL (e.g., localhost or an IP address), not a text editor modifying database files directly or mocked HTML pages.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original site title is 'My WordPress Blog'. The original tagline is 'A WordPress blog for testing and demonstrations'. The correct timezone is 'America/Los_Angeles'. The rogue administrator account injected during setup is named 'service_worker'. The agent must fix 7 specific issues using only built-in settings: blogname spam, blogdescription spam, rogue admin user, plain permalinks, comment moderation disabled, open registration as admin, and timezone changed.
